title: WordPress Site Migration Project Completion Report
summary: The WordPress Site Migration project has been successfully completed, delivering all 27 tracked issues across 6 milestones and culminating in a production deployment on January 29, 2026. The project was executed on an accelerated timeline of 17 days, meeting the critical deadline for NADA conference alignment.
text:
STROLID
WordPress Site Migration
Project Completion Report
Prepared for: Executive Leadership Report Date: January 29, 2026 Project Status: COMPLETE
Executive Summary
The WordPress Site Migration project has been successfully completed. All 27 tracked issues across 6 milestones were delivered,
culminating in a production deployment on January 29, 2026. The project was executed on an accelerated timeline of 17 days,
meeting the critical deadline for NADA conference alignment.
Metric Value
Total Issues 27
Completed 27 (100%)
Project DurationJanuary 13 - 29, 2026
Priority Urgent
Project Lead Michael Donovan
Project Scope
The initiative addressed four primary objectives:
1. New Content Creation - Convert source content (PDF) into WordPress pages using Elementor templates
2. Legacy Migration - Extract, transform, and integrate existing site content via WordPress API
3. Content Deduplication - Identify and reconcile 172+ pages to eliminate redundancy
4. UI/UX Refinement - Standardize navigation, mobile experience, forms, and page consistency
5. Quality Assurance - Structured testing framework with stakeholder feedback loop
6. Production Deployment - Environment configuration, domain migration, and go-live
Milestone Completion Summary
1. New Content Page Creation
Status: COMPLETE
ID Task Owner Completed
STR-5 Standardize content: PDF to MarkdownM. Donovan Jan 27
STR-6 Convert Page MD to Elementor JSON templateM. Donovan Jan 27
STR-7 Import Elementor JSON templates per pageM. Donovan Jan 27
STR-8 Preview Live - Initial function test per pageM. Donovan Jan 27
Outcome: Established a repeatable content pipeline from PDF source material through Markdown standardization to Elementor-
compatible JSON templates, enabling rapid page creation.
2. Legacy Content Migration
Status: COMPLETE
ID Task Owner Completed
STR-9 Site Content Export - All Pages (WP API) P. Trovao Jan 27
STR-10 Convert MD to JSON Elementor P. Trovao Jan 27
STR-11 Import JSON Elementor Pages M. Donovan Jan 27
STR-12 Page-level Integration P. Trovao Jan 27
STR-13 Preview Live - Initial Function Test P. Trovao Jan 27
STR-14 Add to existing pages vs. creating new pagesP. Trovao Jan 27
Outcome: All legacy content was exported via WordPress API, converted to the new template format, and integrated into the unified
site structure. The team chose to update existing pages rather than create new ones, preserving SEO metadata (titles, descriptions,
URLs).
3. Deduplication of Legacy and New Content
Status: COMPLETE
ID Task Owner Completed
STR-15 Discover and Designate: 172 Total Pages P. Trovao Jan 27
STR-16 Reconciliation: Phase 1 - Identify duplicatesM. Donovan Jan 27
STR-17 Redirect Conflicts and Loops M. Donovan Jan 27
STR-18 Manual Low-level Reconciliation P. Trovao Jan 27
Outcome: The full page inventory (172 pages) was audited. Draft/unpublished pages were removed from scope. Redirect conflicts
and loops were resolved. Side-by-side comparison eliminated duplicate content while preserving SEO-valuable pages.
4. UI/UX Refinement
Status: COMPLETE
ID Task Owner Priority Completed
STR-19 Page-level Consistency (CTA/FAB, layout, CSS)P. Trovao High Jan 27
STR-20 Navigation Menu Final Updates P. Trovao High Jan 27
STR-21 Resource Center: Taxonomy Filtering P. Trovao High Jan 29
STR-22 Responsive: Mobile Navigation Menu P. Trovao High Jan 27
STR-23 Solutions Mega Menu Reorder P. Trovao High Jan 27
STR-25 Phone Number Tap-to-Call Feature P. Trovao High Jan 27
STR-26 Responsive: Mobile Header Tap-to-Call (FAB) M. Donovan Urgent Jan 28
STR-27 Sales BDC Page: Random Scroll Effect FixP. Trovao Urgent Jan 27
STR-28 Zoho Form: Employment Page P. Trovao Urgent Jan 28
STR-29 Zoho Form: Contact Us Page P. Trovao Urgent Jan 28
STR-30 Configure Styling for Cards M. Donovan High Jan 29
Outcome: Site-wide visual and functional consistency was achieved. Navigation was restructured per executive direction (Sales BDC
and Service BDC prioritized in Solutions menu). Mobile experience was optimized with responsive navigation and tap-to-call
functionality. Zoho form integrations were completed and tested. Resource Center taxonomy filtering enables category-based
content discovery.
5. Staging Testing
Status: COMPLETE
ID Task Owner Completed
STR-24 Create Testing Framework M. Donovan Jan 29
STR-31 Revisions and Updates (Test Group Feedback)P. Trovao Jan 29
Outcome: A structured QA framework was established (Google Doc/Sheet/Form) for testers to submit feedback by URL, status,
category, and suggestions. All revisions from the test group were incorporated prior to production deployment.
6. Security Hardening / Production Deployment
Status: COMPLETE
ID Task Owner Completed
STR-32 Environment Configuration: WP EngineM. Donovan Jan 29
STR-33 Domain Configuration M. Donovan Jan 29
STR-34 Deploy to Production M. Donovan Jan 29
STR-35 Lighthouse Report (Baseline) M. Donovan Jan 29
Outcome: Production environment was configured in WP Engine alongside the staging instance to support a proper development
workflow. DNS was migrated with strolid.com as the primary domain (including www). Production deployment was executed with
cache clearing. A Lighthouse baseline report was generated to inform post-launch performance optimization priorities.
Team Performance
Team Member Issues Completed Primary Focus Areas
Paulo Trovao 15 Content migration, UI/UX refinement, page reconciliation, testing revisions
Michael Donovan 12 Content pipeline, environment setup, domain configuration, deployment
Deliverables Summary
Production Website: Live at strolid.com
Content Pipeline: PDF → Markdown → Elementor JSON → WordPress
Page Inventory: Consolidated from 172 pages to deduplicated, SEO-optimized structure
Mobile Experience: Responsive navigation, tap-to-call FAB, optimized header
Form Integrations: Zoho forms on Employment and Contact Us pages
Resource Center: Category-based filtering for blog content
Testing Framework: Structured stakeholder feedback collection process
Lighthouse Baseline: Performance benchmark established for future optimization
Post-Launch Recommendations
1. Performance Optimization - Review Lighthouse baseline report and prioritize improvements for Core Web Vitals
2. SEO Monitoring - Track organic traffic and rankings over the next 30-60 days to confirm no negative impact from
migration
3. Redirect Audit - Conduct a final sweep to confirm no residual 404s or redirect loops
4. Content Roadmap - Develop new content aligned with AI positioning (Viconic, VCON, conversational data expertise)
Project Status: COMPLETE
Generated from Linear project data - WordPress Site Migration (STR) Report Date: January 29, 2026
